mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in subsequent 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized household demand growth, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a monthly increase in the industrial production index reflects resilient manufacturing output that offsets higher borrowing costs, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods correlate with negative 5-day forward returns, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently coincides with negative 20-day forward returns, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation months produce positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases, or the threshold signal shows no predictive edge with Rank IC below 0.02 over a 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates an economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive forward returns in greater than 55 percent of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 0.5 percent signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which improves corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Strong industrial production months are followed by negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.02 in rolling 3-year tests.
